items.title: Spot Prices Wrap: Henry Hub, AECO Climb as Rattled Global Market Calls on U.S. LNG
items.summary: Physical natural gas trading for day-ahead delivery was mixed on Thursday, with losses in the Northeast and Permian Basin offsetting modest gains elsewhere amid mostly bearish domestic fundamentals.

items.title: Futures Settle: U.S. Natural Gas Prices Follow Global Benchmarks Higher as Iran War Takes Toll
items.summary: Natural gas futures strengthened on Thursday, with the 2026/27 winter strip posting sharper gains than nearby months as markets mulled the long-term impacts of an intensifying Middle East war.

items.title: MidDay Snapshot: Storage Gains, Regional Weather Changes Split Spot Natural Gas Prices
items.summary: Weather-driven demand shifts once again fractured the spot market, with natural gas prices for Friday delivery posting uneven gains and losses across regions.

items.title: Ontario Natural Gas Storage Poised for Strong Injection Season Amid Wide Price Spreads
items.summary: Ontario natural gas storage levels are narrowly outpacing last year but remain significantly below the five-year average, setting the stage for what could be one of the most aggressive injection seasons in years.

items.title: MidDay Futures: EIA Injection Caps Gains, But Global Tensions Keep Natural Gas Supported
items.summary: April Nymex natural gas futures eased from early highs after the U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA) reported a 35 Bcf storage injection for the week ended March 13. Still, the contract found support from escalating global tensions tied to t

items.title: Qatar Outage May Deliver Windfall for U.S. LNG Exporters Amid Price Surge, Supply Fears
items.summary: Global natural gas markets were roiled after news of widespread damage and the possibility of a prolonged outage in Qatar, sparking long-term implications for demand from key LNG markets.

items.title: Midmorning Markets: Middle East Crisis Trumps EIA Natural Gas Storage Injection
items.summary: The U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA) on Thursday reported a 35 Bcf injection into natural gas storage for the week ended March 13. The result marked the first net inventory increase of the season and proved bearish relative to historical

items.title: QatarEnergy Says Damage at LNG Facilities Could Take Years to Repair, Upending Supply Outlook
items.summary: QatarEnergy warned Thursday it would take up to five years to repair damage to its LNG facilities after two separate missile attacks by Iran hit its Ras Laffan Industrial City.

items.title: Cuba's Fragile Power Grid Finds a Powerful New Partner
items.summary: Cuba finally reconnected its power supply on Tuesday after an extended blackout swept the island as the United States tried to choke off the island’s energy supply. Blackouts lasted nearly 30 hours as Donald Trump engineered an oil blockade and publi

items.title: US: No Ban On Oil Exports
items.summary: The Trump administration is drawing a clear line as oil prices surge: no export ban. With Brent crude pushing $110 per barrel and U.S. gasoline prices nearing $4 a gallon, pressure is building in Washington to respond to the fallout from the Iran war

items.title: Qatar LNG Hit Turns Into Multi-Year Crisis
items.summary: Qatar’s LNG outage will span years. Repairs to damage at Qatar’s massive Ras Laffan complex will take three to five years to complete, according to QatarEnergy CEO Saad al-Kaabi, cited by Reuters, turning what markets initially treated as a wartime d

items.title: India Unveils Ambitious Plan to Quadruple Solar Power by 2035
items.summary: India expects to nearly quadruple its solar power capacity and triple wind power-generating assets within ten years, according to the new Generation Adequacy Plan published by the country’s Central Electricity Authority.  India projects to have a tot

items.title: Why Electrification Is Europe’s Only Real Hedge Against Oil Shocks
items.summary: Tensions in the Strait of Hormuz are still rising, and Europe is still falling back on the same instinct: worry about supply, brace for higher prices, and revive talk of more domestic oil and gas production. It is an understandable reflex. It is also

items.title: Japan’s Jera Sees Iran War Pushing LNG Buyers to U.S. and Canada
items.summary: More buyers could be pushed to seek and contract LNG supply from producers outside the Middle East, such as the U.S. and Canada, if a prolonged war continues to choke supply from the Gulf region, a senior executive at Japan’s biggest LNG buyer, Jera,

items.title: Asia Turns to U.S. Oil as War Chokes Middle East Supply
items.summary: U.S. crude loadings for Asia are set for a three-year in April as Asian refiners are accelerating buying of American oil to partially offset the loss of supply from the Middle East, traders with knowledge of recent purchases told Bloomberg on Thursda
